commit.message:
release: v1.0.2 (#29)

* chore: update branching strategy and CI for release workflow

- CONVENTIONS.md: replace branch strategy with release-branch model
  (fix/* → release/X.Y.x → main, long-lived release branches)
- CI: add release/** to push and pull_request triggers
- Add version bump advisory and GitHub Releases policy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both
LimitCreate and LimitUpdate ensures the stored value always maps cleanly
to a whole percentage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: draw continuous average trend line across sparse historical data

Use null instead of omitting the average property so Recharts
connectNulls draws a continuous dashed line through gaps. Also guard
the tooltip against null values to avoid showing misleading \.

Adds backend test documenting _average_series behavior with empty
periods (months with zero expenses are excluded from the average).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: limit Insights transactions to first page with View all link (#27)

Remove infinite scroll from the Insights transaction panel. Only show
the first page (~20 items) and add a View all link that navigates to
/transactions with the current filters applied.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: send category filters in correct schema format when creating limits (#23)

Frontend was sending category_ids (ignored by Pydantic) instead of
filters: [{filter_type, filter_value}]. Also fixed edit form loading
f.id (filter record UUID) instead of f.filter_value (category UUID).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: extend trend line to full period and use 1-based days (#26)

_to_cumulative now accepts period_days so the current series extends
to the end of the month/week even when there are no recent expenses.
get_day_of_period returns 1-based indices so the X-axis starts at
day 1 instead of misleading day 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Update docstring and ARCHITECTURE.md to reflect 1-based day index
- Use public get_day_of_period instead of private _resolve_local_date
- Cap period_days as hard limit (ignore timezone-shifted outliers)

commit.message:
Auto-correct file paths missing project subdirectory prefix

- ConflictDetector.ResolvePathsAsync: matches AI-generated short paths
  (e.g., Components/Header.razor) against the repo tree and rewrites them
  to the correct full path (src/MyProject/Components/Header.razor)
- InferProjectPrefix: when no exact suffix match exists, finds the most
  common project root among repo files sharing the same directory name
- PullRequestWorkflow.ResolveFilePathsAsync: exposes path resolution for
  both local workspace and GitHub API commit flows
- EngineerAgentBase: resolves paths after parsing AI output, before writing
  to workspace or committing via API
- Conflict detection now runs AFTER path resolution for fewer false warnings

commit.message:
fix(configure-live): create /var/usrlocal before using /usr/local

/usr/local is a symlink to ../var/usrlocal (bootc layout). bootc-rootfs.sh
wipes /var so the symlink target is missing. mkdir -p through a broken
symlink returns exit 1. Recreate /var/home and /var/usrlocal at script start.

Feat: add logic/ICD error reporting alongside spelling errors

Doctors can now report two types of issues:
- Spelling errors (yazim xetasi): corrected text for diagnosis/explanation fields
- Logic/ICD errors (mentiq xetasi): description of ICD coding problems

Admin panel shows both types with distinct badges and layouts.

fix(vite): allowedHosts: true — allow all reverse-proxy hosts

Vite 8 blocks requests with non-matching Host header even with specific
allowedHosts list, because Tailscale Serve rewrites the Host header.
Using allowedHosts: true is safe here since host is bound to 127.0.0.1
(not accessible from external network directly).

fix: load product.json at bootstrap to enable marketplace search

The Tauri bootstrap did not set _VSCODE_PRODUCT_JSON before importing
workbench modules, causing product.ts to fall through to a hardcoded
default without extensionsGallery configuration. This meant the
Extension Gallery service was never enabled, and marketplace search
returned no results.

Add a get_product_json Rust command that reads product.json and
package.json from the project root, and call it in the bootstrap
script to set the globals before any workbench module is loaded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace DEFAULT_VARIANT_SENTINEL with asset-name template matching

The sentinel approach was broken for packages like uv and copilot-cli
where extract_variant_from_asset returns non-None values (e.g. '64', 'apple',
'gh-copilot') for default binaries due to imperfect platform pattern stripping.

Replace with normalize_asset_for_matching() which strips version numbers
and compares normalized asset names directly, bypassing extract_variant_from_asset
entirely for the no-named-variant update case.

Fixes: 'Variant (default) no longer available' error for uv, copilot-cli,
ripgrep, and any other package with unusual asset naming.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: switch imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough (#72)

* Initial plan

* fix: change imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough

The IMAGES Worker binding (env.IMAGES) is not provisioned — Cloudflare
Images is a paid add-on that has not been enabled for this account.
Using 'cloudflare-binding' caused env.IMAGES to be undefined at runtime,
which broke env.ASSETS.fetch() and produced a 'Page not found' fallback
on every request.

'passthrough' skips image optimisation entirely and passes images through
unchanged, which is the correct behaviour for a static site served from
Workers Assets where Cloudflare Images is not provisioned.

Updated the comment above imageService to document:
- why 'passthrough' is used
- what is required before switching to 'cloudflare-binding' in future

No IMAGES binding exists in src/types/env.ts — no change needed there.

commit.message:
fix: refresh equity immediately after manual trade close

POST /api/trades/<id>/close was closing the trade and recording PnL in
the trades table but never updating bot_status.equity. The equity KPI
reads from bot_status, so it stayed at the initial \ until the next
background price-sync cycle (5 min) or candle tick (15 min).

Fix: after db.close_trade(), recalculate and persist equity in bot_status
right away — for paper trading from the DB totals, for live trading by
delegating to the running bot's _refresh_equity_after_close(). This
mirrors the behaviour already present in the bot's internal close path.

Add five regression tests in TestManualCloseTrade to verify:
- equity is updated immediately on a paper-mode manual close
- equity accumulates correctly across multiple manual closes
- 404 / 400 / 502 error paths still behave correctly

Bump addon version to 1.3.3 for Home Assistant update detection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: ZFS root support in live ISO

- Add --add zfs to live dmsquash-live initramfs so ZFS pools are accessible
  in the live session (requires zfs-dracut installed in base image)
- Deploy zfs-install script to /usr/local/bin/zfs-install in the live image:
  partitions disk (EFI + ZFS), creates rpool with lz4/posixacl/xattr-sa
  datasets (root, var, home), mounts under /mnt/target, runs
  bootc install to-filesystem with root=ZFS=rpool/root kargs
- Add --encrypt flag for aes-256-gcm native ZFS encryption (stretch goal)
- Wire into configure-live.sh; add placeholder desktop entry

Closes #1
